Parameter: Effect size: % reduction in RHD risk with prevention program
Source / notes: From Cuba observational study (Pinar del Rio 1986-1996)
Evidence base is observational, not RCT. Unchanged from original.
Cuba→India extrapolation. Cuba has universal free healthcare; India does not. Unchanged from original.

Parameter: % of over-20 deaths caused by RHD in India
Source / notes: GBD 2021: ~166,017 India RHD deaths / ~10M all-cause deaths. Original used 1.50% (129k deaths).

Parameter: Lifetime RHD deaths per cohort of 100,000
Lifetime deaths averted per 100,000 with prevention program
Units of value per RHD death averted (GW moral weights)
Source / notes: GiveWell moral weights for LMIC adult death. Unchanged.

Parameter: Morbidity uplift: YLDs averted from RHD (% of mortality UoV)
Source / notes: I'm assuming +30%. RHD causes chronic heart failure, reduced exercise tolerance, and need for lifelong secondary prophylaxis. This is a rough guess — not verified against GBD YLD data.

Parameter: Perinatal outcomes uplift (% of mortality UoV)
Source / notes: I'm assuming +15%. RHD is 'the principal heart disease seen in pregnant women' per WHO; causes maternal and perinatal morbidity/mortality. Rough guess.
Calculation: mortality UoV x (1 + morbidity + perinatal)

Parameter: Annual program cost in 2010 USD (Watkins et al. 2015)
Source / notes: From Cuba study. No new cost data found. GiveWell considered this 'possibly a gross underestimate'.
I'm estimating ~1.46 for 2010→2026. Should verify with BLS CPI calculator.

I'm assuming 3x. Cuba has universal free healthcare with existing primary care infrastructure; India would need to build screening, treatment, and referral systems. 3x is moderate — at 5x the CE is still ~16x (above bar). At 1x (original) CE is ~54x (almost certainly too high).

Parameter: Adjusted annual program cost in 2026 USD
Number covered by program annually (Watkins et al. 2015)

Source / notes: Calculation (mortality only; does not include morbidity/perinatal)
Best-guess estimate. Includes 3x cost multiplier and ad-hoc benefit adjustments. At 5x cost multiplier → ~16x. Confidence: Low (cost is pivotal unknown).
